phrases.text_local: À quelle heure commencent les visites guidées ?
phrases.pronunciation: ah kel UR koh-MAHNS lay vee-ZEET ghee-DAY ?
phrases.cultural_note: Guided historical tours ('visites guidées') are a beloved French pastime — France has thousands of classified historic monuments, and the French take heritage tourism very seriously.

responses.french: Les visites guidées commencent à dix heures et à quatorze heures.
responses.english: Guided tours start at ten o'clock and at two o'clock.
responses.phonetic: lay vee-ZEET ghee-DAY koh-MAHNS ah deez UR ay ah kah-TORZ UR.

responses.french: La prochaine visite est dans vingt minutes.
responses.english: The next tour is in twenty minutes.
responses.phonetic: lah pro-SHEN vee-ZEET ay dahn van mee-NOOT.

phrases.text_local: Ce domaine est vraiment magnifique. Quelle splendeur !
phrases.pronunciation: suh doh-MEN ay vray-MAHN mah-nyee-FEEK. kel splahn-DUR !
phrases.cultural_note: 'Un domaine' in French often refers to a wine estate, but it beautifully fits a grand historic property like this. The word carries a sense of aristocratic elegance.

phrases.intent_en: Ask about Henry Clay's connection to France
phrases.text_local: Est-ce qu'Henry Clay avait des liens avec la France ?
phrases.pronunciation: ess-KUH ON-ree CLAY ah-VAY day LYAN ah-VEK lah FRAHNS ?
phrases.cultural_note: Henry Clay negotiated with French diplomats during the early republic and was deeply influenced by French Enlightenment thought. This makes for a genuinely interesting historical conversation.

responses.french: Oui, Henry Clay était fasciné par la politique française et admirait Napoléon.
responses.english: Yes, Henry Clay was fascinated by French politics and admired Napoleon.
responses.phonetic: WEE, ON-ree CLAY ay-TAY fah-see-NAY par lah poh-lee-TEEK frahn-SEZ ay ah-mee-RAY nah-poh-lay-OHN.

phrases.text_local: Les jardins sont superbes. Est-ce qu'ils sont d'époque ?
phrases.pronunciation: lay zhar-DAN sohn soo-PAIRB. ess-KEEL sohn day-POK ?
phrases.cultural_note: Formal gardens ('les jardins à la française') were a hallmark of French design influence worldwide. Asking if gardens are original ('d'époque') shows genuine historical curiosity.

responses.french: Les jardins ont été restaurés selon les plans d'origine du dix-neuvième siècle.
responses.english: The gardens were restored according to the original nineteenth-century plans.
responses.phonetic: lay zhar-DAN ohn ay-tay res-toh-RAY suh-LOHN lay PLAN doh-ree-ZHEEN doo deez-NUV-yem SYEKL.

phrases.text_local: Je souhaiterais faire la visite guidée. Comment puis-je réserver ?
phrases.pronunciation: zhuh swet-uh-RAY fair lah vee-ZEET ghee-DAY. koh-MAHN pwee-zhuh ray-zair-VAY ?
phrases.cultural_note: Using 'je souhaiterais' (I would like / I wish to) instead of 'je veux' (I want) is a mark of polished French — it's the phrasing used when you want to sound cultured and considerate.

responses.french: Vous pouvez acheter votre billet à l'accueil, juste à l'entrée.
responses.english: You can buy your ticket at the welcome desk, just at the entrance.
responses.phonetic: voo poo-VAY ah-shuh-TAY votr bee-YAY ah lah-KWHY, zhoos ah lahn-TRAY.

responses.french: La prochaine visite guidée part dans dix minutes.
responses.english: The next guided tour leaves in ten minutes.
responses.phonetic: lah pro-SHEN vee-ZEET ghee-DAY par dahn dee mee-NOOT.
